entries.denialText: KYM GULLY Page 1 of 2 Date prepared August 29, 2024 Notice of loss date August 21, 2024 Claim number 957275-GP Policy number 5345HR180153 Questions? Contact Claims Associate Thomes Buchanan FL Lic. W816843 nationwide-claims@nationwide.com PO BOX 182068 COLUMBUS, OH 43218-2068 Phone 866-425-9200 x15650 KYM GULLY Fax 866-240-7178 23037 OLYMPIA DR BRAMBLETON, VA 20148-5666 DENIAL OF COVERAGE We've made a decision about this Dwelling Dear KYM GULLY, claim We’ve reviewed the available information for this Dwelling claim. We have completed our investigation of your claimed loss, including a thorough review of your Nationwide Insurance Company Of America Homeowners policy. We must respectfully advise you that your policy does not provide coverage for this loss – as further detailed below. Claim details Insurer: Nationwide Insurance Company Of America Policyholder: KYM GULLY Claim number: 957275-GP Loss date: April 15, 2024 About our decision Our review showed that the damages to your property is a result of wear, tear, and deterioration. After review of the facts of this loss, along with the pertinent policy language detailed below, we respectfully deny coverage for this claim. Policy details Your Homeowners policy and any applicable endorsements state, in relevant part, the following: I - PROPERTY COVERAGES A. Coverage A — Dwelling 1. We cover: a. The dwelling on the "residence premises" shown in the Declarations, including structures attached to the dwelling; and b. Materials and supplies located on or next to the "residence premises" used to construct, alter, or repair the dwelling or other structures on the "residence premises". 2. We do not cover land, including land on which the dwelling is located, or the replacement, rebuilding, restoration, stabilization, or value of such land. SECTION I — PERILS INSURED AGAINST A. Coverage A — Dwelling And Coverage B — Other Structures KYM GULLY Claim # 957275-GP Page 2 of 2 1. We insure against direct physical loss to property described in Coverages A and B. 2. We do not insure, however, for loss: a. Excluded under Section I — Exclusions; b. Involving collapse, including any of the following conditions of property or any part of the property: (1) An abrupt falling down or caving in; (2) Loss of structural integrity, including separation of parts of the property or property in danger of falling down or caving in; or (3) Any cracking, bulging, sagging, bending, leaning, settling, shrinkage or expansion as such condition relates to (1) or (2) above; except as provided in E.B. Collapse under Section I — Property Coverages; or c. Caused by: (6) Any of the following: (a) Wear and tear, marring, deterioration; Additional information We expressly reserve all other rights, defenses, or contentions, which are available to us under the policy of insurance, by law or otherwise, and do not waive any such rights or defenses which we now have or which may become known to us in the future. If you have information about this claim that may affect our current decision, please forward it to us as soon as possible. For more information If you have any questions or concerns about your claim, please contact me at 866-425-9200 x15650 or nationwide-claims@nationwide.com. Sincerely, Thomes Buchanan FL Lic. W816843 Nationwide Insurance Company Of America PO BOX 182068 COLUMBUS, OH 43218-2068 It is a crime to knowingly provide false, incomplete or misleading information to an insurance company for the purpose of defrauding the company.?Penalties include imprisonment, fines and denial of insurance benefits.

Just got off a call with @Ian Thrash he’s in an adjuster meeting with Homesite and he said the adjuster never covers hail damage to soft metals for Homesite. I explained to him that with Homesite the policy has to have the cosmetic endorsement for hail damage to be covered to soft metals on the policy. It’s pretty rare for the policy to have it so I would steer away from hail damage to soft metals with Homesite. That doesn’t mean they won’t cover hail damage to shingles though, so in that case collateral damage still would help the case. This is mainly for the new guys.

You should be good. I personally have homesite through geico and it isn’t part of the policy normally. I would have had to opt in for a cosmetic exclusion. And it’s been the same with all of our customers that think they aren’t covered for cosmetic damage to siding/metals.

It’s actually the other way around, they purposefully explain it incorrectly. Every homesite policy has an option for a cosmetic exclusion. But it’s still not very common. But they will claim every policy does not cover cosmetic damage. You just have to have the customer confirm if they opted in for that exclusion or not.

Only thing is with homesites policy’s they don’t buy hail damage to metals unless the cosmetic endorsement is present on the policy which is rare AF and a lot of the time the sign up has to be put to halt and the endorsement added and would have to wait for another hailstorm. Better taking the chance hoping it’s there since the claim is already in. This is for future reference for anyone signing up hail damage to metals with homesite

He told me “I’m with Statefarm, they denied me and I had three different contractors come out for multiple re-inspections fighting the claim and they continued to deny me” I told him “sir I see no patches up there and that tells me that those other contractors don’t know how to win a claim with Statefarm, the only way to overcome a partial, which I assume you were given with the missing shingles, is to have a shingle removed for Statefarm to pick up & be ran through their RLS process” I explained further what this process was and he decided he trusted me and the confidence I gave about the process and gave me a shot. Good morning team, just some food for thought. If you are so set in stone on something being hail damaged such as siding but the adjuster is saying that it’s mechanical simply request an engineer be sent out to the property. I had a claim where hail damage was denied twice with field inspectors and I know absolutely 100% forsure that There was hail all over that property, but they denied it. I took obnoxiously evident photos of the hail damage and an engineer was sent out and all of the siding was bought. Do not give in if you are positive that there is hail damage at the property just keep fighting that shit

Hey new guys just a tip with homesite/Afics(American family insurance)/progressive all the same umbrella, they rarely cover for hailstone indentations on soft metals. The insured will need something called a “cosmetic endorsement” present on their policy, to be covered for any type of indentations, which technically are considered to be “cosmetic” damage, which means a minor scratch or dent does nothing with preventing the material from doing what it was built to do. So if you come across these insurance company’s be aware. Homesite and Afics have had policies like this for years and it is stupid rare for the insured to have that endorsement very unlikely. You’ll need holes in the siding or blown out panels to be honored coverage for siding 99% of the time with any of these carriers.

Interestingly enough, I had a ladder assist tell me a couple days ago that whenever we get a Donegal engineer, to wait until he/she is in site and have the HO ask the engineer if they mind having the contractor record them. He said Donegal will say they do mind bc they are supposed to do a brittle test in which they tear up the roof. At that point, if we have them on record, they will have just bought the roof. He said have the HO stand firm on the recording, and the engineer will leave. The insurance company will then send a different engineer out that would be more willing to work with you. He said Donegal is their go to when they want the roof denied. Just food for thought.
Real talk!

I just had a Adjuster from Travelers Hunter, who was arguing that The Hail storm was .5 inches and the roof was a clean denial. I sent him evidence of two storms in the area with different storm dates that were 1 inch hail. He came out and approved the roof Yesterday.

Husband was denied worked on his own. Stressed how important it is to have a contractor and that he didn’t need to file a new claim. He said he’ll touch base on Monday hopefully locked in
Had that happen a couple

Travelers approved this roof. The metal roof and the gutters and downspout were not approved. They have added a cosmetic clause to some of their policies. No cosmetic damage to metal will be paid for. The hail has to be large enough to go through the metal. FYI

Been sending out texts and emails like these lately to past customers who were denied or given partials in the past over two years ago in the northern VA area and was able to bag a few go back and try agains. Only this time I’m far more experienced in the field. These customers I also sent their location under the hail swath to show they were hit and asked if they would be open to me trying again and this is one of the responses. This is one from MD but I got approvals on this area recently and she’s USAA. Got mothers in vienna and one in McLean LFG Food for thought
Fully approved-Allstate
35 SQ Full approval from Travelers, no metal roof or downs. Policy excludes “cosmetic damage.” Hail has to be large enough to puncture metal. Lol

Make sure you stay on these adjusters. after sending my report and arguments/extra photos and hail recon etc, getting the full denial and denials for a second inspection multiple times, The homeowners finally had enough and started calling the insurance to complain and ask questions from supervision. Now there’s a new adjuster on the claim, and the first thing he tells the homeowners was that they made their decision to originally deny because there was only a single photo of the roof. Then I’m like, did their inspector only submit a single photo? I know I had way more in my report that I sent.

Hes been good for me. He is Muslim so wish him Ramadan Mubarak (Happy Ramadan). Get off on the right foot. I had .5 in hail, and other homes in the same community were denied. He chalked my roof up for me and fully approved it.
I’m dealing with the same issue with Allstate. All collateral damage is from hail but the roof is “wear and tear” Make it make sense !!!smh 🤡🤡
Unless it’s NW cause NW chugs boners….had him on a NW crushed and failed BT and missing shingles and they denied but HO is on board to go to war so we’ll get it bought
I have never had a homeowner call me upset that we put a claim in. I’ve had very little damage get approved compared to a demolished roof getting denied! We fight them all the same! We win majority!

I talked to the husband, the wife owns the house. She is at work. She has tried through insurance for the August storm. She got denied by Allstate. I explained that all I have to do is submit a new photo report since her neighbors got approved. She was not budging but after being tactful she said she will call me back. I really wanted to close that. The husband supported me and she said she was busy and will discuss it later. Got to lock this one in
First time I’ve had a claim denied on spot at inspection. Dude claims no storm damage. But down the street the same dude approved a house for full replacement?

Regardless what this weather looks like, let’s make the most of it and find a way to be successful. Let’s build our pipelines when the weather is clear, let’s make sure we are fighting our partials and denials, and let’s push to get in all the estimates we’re waiting on. Don’t let this time pass with a lot of “I wish I did this or that.” Be able to look back when the weather is clear and be able to say you didn’t leave anything on the table. And as it only gets easier from as it warms up and days get longer, let’s take advantage of the better days by remembering what it was like during a time like this. 2025 is going to be a monumental one 🤩

Erie plays the denial card more times than they should after the first inspection. Having a Ho actively fighting for their claim is exactly what you need. Emailing Erie as a contractor is almost detrimental. That’s why we always want our Ho’s bought all the way in with full transparency!
Full approval, USAA full denial first go around not one shingle fought this bad boy to the death came back fully approved second go around 53K estimate
Originally a full denial turned into a full approval for siding only! LFG!!! Travelers. $32k 🫡🫡🫡 what a nice Christmas present 😍😍😍
Massive shout out to @Reese Samala @AmberCouch
Just want to shout out every1 digging deep in the cold. Embrace the cold, embrace the pain. Embrace the denials. Find comfort in it

OH SHIT RAW FOOTAGE OF SHINGLE FLOPPING! Hahaha like rare game hunting hahaha damn how bad I would love to send that to an adjuster that denied that roof. I’d be like “it’s only going to take one good gust of wind and now there are more missing shingles” send me that video I want it lol

This client had no contractor. Was denied coverage. Right in the 2” inch Sterling storm zone. Collateral was so bad his grill was smashed. We did detailed photo report & had @Danny Ticktin write and estimate.
The closest you are to a storm date the best chances you have of turning a claim over. And with the impending freezing additional damage can surface that could lead to potentially being denied

@Mike Swearingen Also flipped that one after getting a FULL DENIAL. With just a GREAT photo report and a tactful and precise email. That’s what it takes yall! Won’t be the same for every job, some will have more steps. But keep getting better at what we do, and you’ll see the benefits! Good photos of STORM DAMAGE are key!!
HO is threatening the adjuster going legal because they denied him (Brendan Myer) and claiming hail didn’t occur there @Ross Renzi the one that’s neighbors with your sign up two days ago
That specific client got denied a year ago. In they were desperate for a new roof & siding. This year we got them 80k in coverage. This is why we do this. Life changing make-over

I literally just emailed USAA about a badou inspection where he marked the same hail hits in all four test squares but they denied any hail damage to the shingles yet covered metal components. My argument was on why the desk adjuster agreed to every other piece of hail
I also had a partial approval for 32k but in the process of trying to flip the roof, just siding and accessories so far but dang feels good to get them in
3rd full approval just came in, HO has battled numerous times with insurance got denied and then got a partial and I took it over and got it flipped. Gotta keep pushing!
Approved- Erie
Full approval Allstate off of a reinspection. HO tries it by herself the first time around, got denied and then chose me to flip it around and BAM! There you go 💪🏽💪🏽💪🏽
Approved-Allstate
Bet that! Imma get the HO to request a new adjuster she didn’t want to work with him cuz he denied her without even getting on the roof xD
@Ross Renzi Is he normally a buyer? He denied a roof that’s super trashed and I took over the claim for a HO since the other company she worked with gave up
Approved-Travelers

I got their next door neighbors on the left and the right fully approved. Also right across the street 2 houses full approvals. I’m not worried about it though, we’ll get a reinspection and get it bought out like I did with his neighbor. Like @Richie says the money is in the fight and it’s standard for first time denials.

If the damage is there he might be fair. Catastrophic damage. Tim has denied numerous asphalt and shingle roofs in my experience. Reschedule and try to get a new Seek now inspector if it’s not catastrophic damage. ￼
A recent denial of mine
Sign-up/Roof/gutters/DS/Allstate/Sorto.

Roof approved USAA. This one has a cool little story behind it. They tried last year with a different roofing company. USAA denied them when I knocked the door. I talked him into trying again because of the storm the same Adjuster came out who denied it last year because it was me and I know Robert Sullivan I have a good relationship with him. The first thing he told me when I saw him was oh it’s you let’s get this thing bought.

For those of you who don’t have many signups this month yet. I never had more than 1-2 signups 5 days in & hit bonus every month. It’s not about how you start, it’s about how you finish. Huge weekend loading… 🔋
Mike Crisafi Just met Joe, told us he is working with you. He is in the hit zone. Loudon Mutual. Get this denial flipped dog, they can't deny it now! Bring a sign with u

Richie Do you have any pictures of both hail and wind damage on extremely old roofs with a lot of wear and tear. It was hard to spot them out. It was an architectural shingle. Had some wind damage but I'm not exactly sure. If anyone else has some pictures feel free to throw em in here
Gotta love when you have collateral like this and a desk adjuster says…. The markings on the shingles look like normal wear and tear…. Not really fooling anyone but themselves👌 time to fight it! 💪
Tried, she said her agent helped her with her car and she trusts her🤷♂️. Spoken to 3 ppl already and smartroof got all 3 denied and ditched them
APPROVED! Travelers.
Approved/ USAA. Been fighting this one for a while. Re inspect happened a month and a half after denial and then it got approved! 💪💪💪

This is the job I mentioned the lady had USAA and a horizon and told me no. I came back the next day and was so happy I came back and signed right up. She was hesitant at first because a few years before she went through the process and got denied.

This was not that hard to overcome. Customer came out, super nice but was really not interested in getting an inspection done because of a previous roofing company that denied there being any damage. I asked as a courtesy for a professional second opinion and BAM. Inspection and sign up.

Positive note 📝 today: I reached out to one of @Reese Samala oldest clients today that was denied this time last year. Looking at the notes that were on file, the HO wanted to try again when another hail storm ⛈️ hit… Well wouldn’t you know this little storm on April 15th was a DiReCt HiT!!! I called him and introduced myself, reminded him of the last conversation he had with Reese and wham bam 💥 we’re going back this week to sign him back up and try again.. moral of this story… TAKE GREAT NOTES AND BE KIND TO YOUR CLIENTS!! 💰💰💰
I knocked on the HO door after our Tuesday meeting to sign him up and the adjuster had done the AM that morning and denied it so he signed with us to fight for him
He denied a HO I signed up 2 weeks ago. Circled about 3 hail hits in each test square when in reality there were 10+. The soft metals were peppered. Definitely take a good photo report
Approved-USAA-Horizon

To give all you guys some perspective on this. This got basically denied first. Mike got it to reinspection, still came back as a partial. Mike pestered the adjuster until he agreed to approve it if the new shingle was a different dimension than the current shingle. Mike goes and picks up a shingle from our storage unit and proves that it’s not the same size. Finally approved.

Started with an 11am adjuster meeting where approved all aluminum siding on the spot and was “checking into policy” as to whether the disco arch shingle would be partially or fully covered. That’s a full for sure!
Here for my appointment call the ho and he come out the house across the street that he also owns. Smartroof worked with him last year full denial said there was 0 hail damage

They will probably know that it’s there then but he was ultimately denied at the end of the day now just make sure to show some good quality damage to the insurance company this time around. I would also make them send me the photo report from the last claim or get the HO to do it so you can have that as backup in case they say the damage you found has already been documented because they love to lie and say that

How is the siding approved for storm damage.. and a 3x3 section approved on the roof for storm damage.. but the rest..? Logically doesn’t make sense? Same Adjuster (Tim from Travlers) that approved the garage of my last house for storm damage but denied the rest of the roof.

Trust me, I tried everything to get him to sign with me letting him know about our reviews. I even showed him our ratings on Google to let him know that we are the best and wanted him to be aware that we are the best that what we do, but he has had three other roofing companies try and has been denied each time, but I told them we are not those other companies

@Edward Tejeda Compere I’ll send you some good repair attempt videos that won roof replacements and all you have to do is replicate what I did 👌 confirm with me that you will be doing this and I’ll then send you the vids to help You out so you aren’t lost out there when you go do it because the repair attempt has to be done absolutely correct or it will 100% be denied

Definitely show the adjuster that if it comes back denied lol be like “ so you’re telling me not enough storm damage but your hired inspector is chalking wood for hail? Yea we need another pair of eyes immediately thank you” lol 😵💫
You’ll gain more clients if you turn a partial or denial from a different company to a full approval. I have flipped so many from different companies

When you have agreed to sign to give us the job for whatever the insurance payout is at the end then we will be present at the adjuster meeting and not only that but we will chalk it all up and get it prepped the day before which the adjusters absolutely love because it just makes their jobs easier. Rather, you are depending on a ladder assist who gets paid a $100 to take photos and leave on to the next one. Do you seriously want your results do be determined by such an inspector? Completely up to you sir. When you are denied or given a small partial which is extremely likely going to happen, we will be here ready for you. Have a great night sir
When you guys know ahead of time that one of these bad ones are assigned, you can get the homeowner to reschedule. With seek now it basically gets randomized again.
Is that common? I have a Nationwide claim, SeekNow adjuster was great and left me feeling super positive for approval, but got full denial from the desk adjuster and the damage was brutal.

I had a Statefarm adjuster just call me. I think her name is Deja. She was basically just calling to tell me that she was on my side and sending the info to management for review to do a reinspect. Point being these adjusters are just people like us. She was very nice. I brought up some of the damages and she basically said she agrees with me and she brought up a different hail date that I didn’t use but was a few moths after. I asked if I could change the original and use that one. She said well let’s see if we can get this reinspected and prove that there’s damages so we don’t have to and we will go from there. LG. That’s a win in my book. Don’t count Statefarm out! The initial estimate is a full denial. I’m going to flip this! It’s all part of the game! 💰

Fellas, this is one thing for the new guys I’d like to put up there about building relationship. This job was a full denial from USAA. I got the homeowner to file for reinspection. They granted it they sent out an IA that I’ve never met. I literally spent two hours on the roof, plucking this guy‘s brain about the industry about hail damage. I played the new guy role and it worked. We literally talked about the industry for two straight hours, sitting on the ridge vent and enjoying the weather and then, after the two hour discussion I had with him, he gets up and says let’s buy this roof and just like that full approval after denial USAA let’s go
Approved USAA!!! 🇺🇸 Two properties, one continuous roof. One side got approved at first and the other got denied and both with USAA. With persistence and a reinspection, it finally got approved. 💪🏽

What helps me is while signing them up make sure they know that the insurance is not on their side. Even if they are best friends with their agent still doesn’t matter. All my clients know insurance are the enemies. Makes it easier to have them on your side to fight partials or denials.
Approved-USAA, denied it the first inspection. Sent in a solid photo report and a line item estimate created by the one in only @AmberCouch 🐐 and got it flipped!

Man my last sign up had so many objections; had two claims already denied within two years and I was able to get them on board. It’s USAA at that. Nothing is deterring. This is what we do. One day can go nowhere and the next you get three signs ups in a day. Never be content. I aim for three every day. One just doesn’t hit the spot right. Push it to the limit.

@Reese Samala That’s right dude i went over this IAs head and sent my photos to the supervisor he reviewed them and boom apologized to me for the IAs decision he denied it 1st but my photos said otherwise- $37k siding job 🙌🏾

I just added him to the adjuster docs. He denied hail damage was associated to the storm we claimed because there was no present spatter marks and that spatter stays present for 18 months and ultimately he denied the claim. I got a report inspection done with a dude named Daniel and Daniel got all the roof and the siding approved -_- like bruh
Approved-USAA-Galliart.. denied first inspection. Submitted an argument with my photo report and moisture readings of the interior damages and boom!
@Christopher Ramirez He’s a laid back guy. He’s not local. He told me there was hail damage. The estimate came back as a full denial. He was my reinspect. He’s young.

Roof and Interior Approved - Travelers /after months of going back and forth, a reinspect that they still denied, and keeping the homeowner bought in with who we were. He argued enough because he knew we were doing the right thing. The last line of his last email was “I’m a financial advisor and I know nothing about roofs. But Reese clearly does and that email was even easy for me to understand. How can you still be denying me the coverage that my policy is supposed to cover?”
@Richie Im almost positive I had him for a claim and had to reach out to his supervisor to get him to answer his email and phone. Also full denied my claim, said everything was thermal blistering.
Approved/ Travelers: Denied after inspection(seek now). Homeowner got involved and the Supervisor allowed a second photo report. Boom!

So yea moral of the story. If you truly feel that you have found some good hail fight tooth and MF nail for that approval because the insurance company sure the hell won’t most of the time. This is exactly why I’ve gotten nearly $4M in revenue in a year and a half because I don’t ever agree with denials

That’s my first ever Chubb approval!! And believe it or not I did a claim with this guy last year and it was his second claim then and second year in a row with the same engineer that came out for the first claim and fully denied twice in a row! I went ahead and asked him if he wanted to give it one more try since there was a newer storm and BOOM!! Fkn full approval $40k Roof only 🤑 free $40k roof for Dominion Jeweler Owner Mr Rojas LFG BABY
You see the difference between the two? The one on the left is a terrible pic of a hail hit. Try to get all the wear and tear out of the photo and focus on the hail hit itself

Sorry guys tried to post a photo report that I did that won a claim that was denied with another contractor and allstate. We really need to do some photo report training I think we should emphasize on that a little bit tomorrow @Oliver Brown 💪

Sent this guy the contingency for esign, he's reluctant to sign since he has a couple of other contractors coming out to give quotes for a partial approval, but I showed him that there's damage across the whole roof and he agreed that he wants a full replacement. So I guess he's going to sleep on it and talk to the other guys then I'll follow up with him for the contingency. He signed the claim auth so that I could reopen the claim for him

She says she’s going to talk to her family first and asked for my card. She said she got denied once in April and then signed with somebody like a month ago but never put in a claim because she mainly wants her pipe Jack fixed and didn’t trust the person she signed with but I told her it would be the same price or most likely cheaper to just go through our process with me. And she says she trusts me so we will see.
That advice could save me a lead that was a denial and a high premium @Richie @Asel Tulesheva
We did just that and one week later BOOM full approved three small sides of siding with 25% 0&P

I said no worries do you have aluminum siding? He said yes but it was denied when I filed the claim for the roof last year. I said great, I'm going to go and check it out, we've had recent hail storms in that area over the summer. He said go ahead. I go there and I swear to you find like five hits on two of the three elevations but was like fuck it a hit looks like a hit regardless of how many and we filed the claim. It ended up being the same adjuster as last year that denied his siding so I was like Fuck. None the less tho, the adjuster contacted me after a detailed photo report was sent to him and asked me to cut a piece of the siding and bring it to him so he can rub it to the lab for verification.
Approved - Architectural roof - travelers - Joe Murphy denied on the inspection and came back himself a week later thought he missed something and approved it!!!
Just a friendly reminder to pleaseeeee get photos of flashing on every job. Still the #1 thing getting denied on supps. Need photos showing it’s damaged or nailed through
Great day to get organized:

Another note for you guys. When photographing damage for help in converting a denial or partial, test squares should be done on all slopes. And totals of each sloped damaged written and photographed. Close ups of damage and wide angles to show where it is. And use chalk always. This makes it easy to see in wide angles. If you need chalk lmk and I will get you some. Thanks.
Lead up for grabs in Germantown. Details below:

entries.source: Susan Maryland Insurance Administration Matching Requirement 2.pdf
entries.denialText: Information about Maryland Insurance Administration Matching Requirement 2
entries.counterText: Failure to adjust such claims in accordancewith policy provisions or the company’s own operations or settlement guidelines may resultin a finding that the insurer is in violati on of Section 230A, Article 48A of the Code.

entries.source: Susan Contingency and Claim Authorization Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Contingency and Claim Authorization Script?
entries.counterText: After the claim: “Okay, perfect! Like they said, an adjuster will be reaching out to you in the next 24 to 48 hours to schedule the inspection. The absolute most important part of this process is that I am at this inspection. Insurance companies don’t want to pay out. They are trying to mitigate their losses after storms. I am there as your representation to make sure you get a fair shake.

entries.source: Susan Contingency and Claim Authorization Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Contingency and Claim Authorization Script?
entries.counterText: “This basic/simple agreement backs you as the homeowner by guaranteeing you that your only cost will be your deductible if we get you fully approved. If it is a partial approval or denial, first we will fight and jump through the necessary hoops to turn that into a full approval; but if we are not able to get you fully approved, this contract is null and void and you do not owe us a penny.”

entries.source: Susan Contingency and Claim Authorization Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Contingency and Claim Authorization Script?
entries.counterText: “What’s in it for us, is we just want to get to do the work. This agreement commits you to using us if we hold up our end of the bargain and achieve a full-approval.”

entries.source: Susan Contingency and Claim Authorization Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Contingency and Claim Authorization Script?
entries.counterText: “This next form is our Claim Authorization form. Very simple, it allows us to communicate with your insurance company. I’ll be here for the inspection and we will also communicate with them through email and phone calls so you don’t have to be a middle-man. Of course, I’ll always keep you looped in with our communication by CCing you in all emails and updating you on any conversations we have.

entries.source: Susan Contingency and Claim Authorization Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Contingency and Claim Authorization Script?
entries.counterText: “Alright, we are all set! Again, the most important part of this process is that I am here when the insurance company comes out. Ideally you can have them call me to schedule that directly. If they call me, great! But, regardless, please get the adjuster’s information (name, email, phone number) and send that over to me so that I can communicate with them before the inspection. If they insist on scheduling with you, go ahead and pencil in a time and avoid these times and days [provide your schedule]

entries.source: Susan Contingency and Claim Authorization Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Contingency and Claim Authorization Script?
entries.counterText: “Thank you, sir/ma’am, looking forward to seeing you on the day of inspection, you have my contact information on my card if you need anything else.

entries.denialText: How to use Post Adjuster Meeting Script?
entries.counterText: This is the conversation you will have with the HO after an adjuster meeting

entries.denialText: How to use Post Adjuster Meeting Script?
entries.counterText: “Great! The Adjuster/Ladder Assist identified all the same damage as me. You will be receiving an email from us shortly saying that you’ve been fully approved. Within a few days you should receive the itemized estimate from the Insurance Company. Once you receive that, you can forward that to me. This is going to include every line item that the insurance company believes is needed to make you whole again. To you this is going to look like gibberish. But, don’t worry, that’s what we’re here for. If for some weird reason the desk adjuster does not write it for a full approval after this inspection, I will take the steps necessary to get it turned around! Any questions on that, sir/ma’am?”

entries.denialText: How to use Post Adjuster Meeting Script?
entries.counterText: “When the insurance company generates their estimate, it will also typically trigger the release of the first payment. The insurance company refers to this payment as the ACV. Which stands for Actual Cash Value. That amount will be used as your down payment. The remaining funds are held by the insurance company until we complete the work. They refer to these remaining funds as Depreciation. Which is not “depreciation” as most would think about it, it’s just their way of holding on to some of the funds to make sure the work gets done.

entries.denialText: How to use Post Adjuster Meeting Script?
entries.counterText: “At the same time our office will be reviewing your entire project along with my photos and the insurance estimate. As I’ve mentioned, insurance companies are always trying to save money. We usually find specific line items that are missing. Most commonly, code items like IWS and Drip Edge. We will supplement for all the items needed to complete your project to code and our manufacturer’s specifications. Don’t worry, like we have agreed to from the beginning, we guarantee you that you would not have to cover any of these costs even if the insurance company were to deny our supplement. [Which does not happen.]

entries.denialText: How to use Post Adjuster Meeting Script?
entries.counterText: “Once our office has completed your job review, which takes 2 - 3 business days, your job will be assigned to one of our Project Coordinators. They will reach out to schedule a project meeting where they will walk you through all of the finer details. This is the fun part where you will get to pick our products and colors. *Of course if you have anything else you would like us to do beyond the insurance approval, your Project Coordinator could price that out for you.*

entries.denialText: How to use Post Adjuster Meeting Script?
entries.counterText: “Your install date will typically be 4 - 6 weeks after your Project Meeting. After you submit that initial payment from the insurance company, we’ll reach out to get your project scheduled!

entries.denialText: How to use Post Adjuster Meeting Script?
entries.counterText: “I know that was a lot of information. The most important thing from here is forwarding the estimate as soon as you get it. Of course if you have any questions in the meantime, please feel free to reach out.

entries.denialText: How to use Post Adjuster Meeting Script?
entries.counterText: “A great way for me to help assist some of your neighbors, would be if I could put up a yard sign! Would you mind if I put one up? Thank you, sir/ma’am!”

entries.denialText: How to use Post Adjuster Meeting Script?
entries.counterText: Submit your Photo Report and Claim Authorization to the adjuster with an effective email. Use Post-AM-Email-Template if you need to.

entries.denialText: How to use Post Adjuster Meeting Script?
entries.counterText: “So, we just finished our inspection. The Adjuster/Ladder Assist will be sending their report to a desk adjuster and will be making a final decision. Once they make a decision, they will write up an itemized estimate and should send that over to you. Once you receive that, please forward that over to me. They can be a bit hard to read, so I’ll be able to walk through all of that with you.

entries.denialText: How to use Post Adjuster Meeting Script?
entries.counterText: “If it comes back as anything other than a full approval, I’ll take the necessary steps to make sure it gets turned around! There are a lot of steps we can take from here, which we can go over once we receive the estimate.

entries.denialText: How to use Post Adjuster Meeting Script?
entries.counterText: “I will go over all this information again once we receive the estimate and have a more clear picture of what the next steps will be.”

entries.denialText: How to use Post Adjuster Meeting Script?
entries.counterText: “Great! So, if you have any questions between now and when you get your estimate, please reach out! And, again, once you receive that from the insurance company, forward that over to me so I can review that with you. Thank you sir/ma’am! Have a great day!”

entries.denialText: How to use Post Adjuster Meeting Script?
entries.counterText: Submit your Photo Report and Claim Authorization to the adjuster with an effective email. Use Post-AM-Email-Template if you need to.

entries.denialText: How to use Initial Pitch Script?
entries.counterText: “Hi, how are you? My Name is ________ with Roof- ER we’re a local roofing company that specializes in helping homeowners get their roof (and siding) replaced, paid for by their insurance!”

entries.denialText: How to use Initial Pitch Script?
entries.counterText: “We’ve had a lot of storms here in Northern Virginia/Philadelphia/Maryland over the past few months that have done a lot of damage!

entries.denialText: How to use Initial Pitch Script?
entries.counterText: “We’re already working with your neighbors. We’ve been able to help them get fully approved through their insurance company to have their roof and/or siding replaced.”

entries.denialText: How to use Initial Pitch Script?
entries.counterText: “Were you home for the storm we had in ___. Wait for answer

entries.denialText: How to use Initial Pitch Script?
entries.counterText: If yes “It was pretty crazy right?! Wait for answer

entries.denialText: How to use Initial Pitch Script?
entries.counterText: If no: “Oh no worries at all, we get that all the time.

entries.denialText: How to use Initial Pitch Script?
entries.counterText: *“We’re working with a lot of your neighbors in the area. We’ve been able to help them get fully approved through their insurance company to have their roof (and siding) replaced.”

entries.denialText: How to use Initial Pitch Script?
entries.counterText: ^“While I’m here, in the neighborhood, I am conducting a completely free inspection to see if you have similar, qualifiable damage. If you do, I’ll take a bunch of photos and walk you through the rest of the process. If you don’t, I wouldn’t want to waste your time, I wouldn’t want to waste mine! I will at least leave giving you peace of mind that you’re in good shape.”

entries.denialText: How to use Initial Pitch Script?
entries.counterText: Once they agree to let you do the inspection:, “Alright! It will take me about 10 - 15 minutes. I’m gonna take a look around the perimeter of your home, then grab the ladder, and take a look at your roof.

entries.denialText: How to use Initial Pitch Script?
entries.counterText: Go in for a handshake. What was your name again? [Their name] great to meet you, again I am (your name). Oh and by the way do you know who your insurance company is”? Wait for their answer, “Great! We work with those guys all the time.”

entries.denialText: How to use Initial Pitch Script?
entries.counterText: “Here’s my card/flier, look us up while I’m conducting the inspection! I will give you a knock when I finish up and show you what I’ve found.”

entries.denialText: How to use Initial Pitch Script?
entries.counterText: Without any hesitation, move into inspection. (See Inspection and Post-Inspection Script for detailed next steps.)

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: §Damage to roof metals and other collateral on the roof

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: §Circle Hail Hits & Slash Wind Damage (close up of each)

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: §Pictures of granules in the gutters or at the bottom of downspouts

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: §Entire inspection shouldn’t take any longer than 15-20 minutes. How thorough of an inspection you should do is a judgment call. Getting enough photos to sell the homeowner is most important. It doesn’t matter how quickly you are able to complete the inspection if you don’t have enough damage-photos for the homeowner to be on board with filing the claim.

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: “Hey _______, so I have a bunch of photos to show you. First I walked around the perimeter of the house to look for collateral damage. I’ll also show you the collateral damage we found on the roof.” Say this while going through through house number and overview photos

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: Start showing the pictures of damage to screens, gutters, downspouts, and soft metals

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: “While this damage functionally isn’t a big deal, it really helps build a story. Think of us like lawyers and this collateral damage is the evidence that builds the case which helps us get the roof (and siding) approved.”

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: “Here are the photos of the damage to your shingles. Anything I have circled means its hail damage (IF there were any wind damaged shingles or missing shingles say:) and anything I have slashed means its wind damage.

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: Remain on a photo of hail damage as you explain the following

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: “This is exactly what we look for when we're looking for hail damage. If you notice, the divot is circular in nature. Even if this damage doesn’t look like a big deal, what happens over time, these hail divots fill with water, freeze…., when water freezes it expands and breaks apart the shingle which will eventually lead to leaks. That is why your insurance company is responsible and your policy covers this type of damage.”

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: Start slowly swiping through all the pictures of hail.

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: Start slowly swiping through all the pictures of hail.

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: If there was wind damage or missing shingles, say the following:

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: “Now here are the wind damaged shingles. You have both shingles that are creased from the wind lifting them up and shingles that have completely been blown off.”

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: Show the pictures of wind damaged and/or missing shingles (if applicable)

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: Now show them pictures of the granules in gutters and downspouts

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: “As you can see here, granules have filled up your gutters. These granules are supposed to be what’s protecting your home. When wind and hail hits your roof, it knocks out these granules which reduces the lifespan of your roof.”

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: “This is very similar to damage to ________’s home and/or the rest of the approvals we’ve gotten in the area”.

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: “With that being said, insurance companies are always looking for ways to mitigate their losses. It’s unfortunate but that’s how they make money. The most important part of this process is that when your insurance company comes out to run their inspection, we are here as storm experts to make sure you as a homeowner get a fair shake. If they are missing anything we make sure they see all the damage that I just showed you.”

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: “What I’m going to do now is run to my car, grab my iPad and we can get this process started”! Go get iPad

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: As you approach back to the house/homeowner ask “Is there a place we could sit down for 5-10 Minutes”?

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: Once you are in the house, spend some time building rapport as you get settled.

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: “Okay, so first I am going to grab some of your basic information for our system. Then we can let your insurance company know about the damagel”.

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: “Do you happen to know your deductible? If not, no big deal at all”!

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: Ask if they have the app or if they have the login to the website.

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: If not, use the Homeowner Information Sheet to prep the HO. As you are writing or typing the information for the HO to use on the call, explain each part to them.

entries.source: Susan Inspection and Post Inspection Script.docx
entries.denialText: How to use Inspection and Post Inspection Script?
entries.counterText: As much as possible, use HO’s phone to make the call

entries.denialText: How to use iTel Template?
entries.counterText: PLEASE DO NOT JUST COPY AND PASTE, PLEASE REVIEW SECTIONS TO BE CHANGED, REMOVED, OR ADDED (i.e., THE USE OF ‘AND/OR’)

entries.denialText: How to use iTel Template?
entries.counterText: PLEASE EDIT THE INFO TO ALIGN WITH YOUR SPECIFIC JOB!!!! PLEASE DELETE ALL INSTRUCTIONS BEFORE SENDING THIS TO YOUR HOMEOWNER. EVERYTHING IN THE BRACKETS [ ] SHOULD BE DELETED BEFORE SENDING

entries.denialText: How to use iTel Template?
entries.counterText: This is [REP NAME] with Roof ER. I am the contractor assisting [CUSTOMER’S NAME]. Attached you will find an iTel report [AND any additional documents if you attach them.]

entries.denialText: [Alicia Janay Williams (Allstate Vehicle and Property Insurance) 2024-07-23] I have attached a copy of your estimate for your records. If there is anything I may have missed or if you receive estimates from contractors that are higher than the Replacement Cost amount on my estimate, please reach out to me right away so I can review for any potential adjustments that may be needed. You may also email a copy of their estimate along with any photos of damage we may have missed to claims@claims.allstate.com with your claim # in the subject line. If the contractor estimates are all within our Replacement Cost amount, then there is no need to reach out beforehand and you can go ahead and schedule your repairs. If it is higher than what we allowed, please submit the contractor's proposal prior to getting any work done so we can review for possible supplement. [Reese Samala (Roof-ER Director) 2024-07-23] Need to do iTel and RA. [Carlos Alfaro (homeowner — drafted) 2024-09-09] Dear Allstate Team, I am writing to formally express my disagreement with the recent decision to close my claim without a full approval. After reviewing the Repair Estimate and iTel Report provided by my contractor, I believe that my roof deserves a second inspection before the matter is finalized. I have been an Allstate member in good standing since 2016, and I am respectfully requesting that this claim be reopened and a reinspection scheduled with a different adjuster. A fresh evaluation will ensure that all aspects of the damage are thoroughly reviewed. I would also like to request a meeting to discuss the decision and any additional facts that may support reconsidering the claim. Please let me know when it would be convenient to arrange this conversation. Thank you for your time and attention to this matter. I look forward to your prompt response.
entries.counterText: Dear Allstate Team, I am writing to formally express my disagreement with the recent decision to close my claim without a full approval. After reviewing the Repair Estimate and iTel Report provided by my contractor, I believe that my roof deserves a second inspection before the matter is finalized. I have been an Allstate member in good standing since 2016, and I am respectfully requesting that this claim be reopened and a reinspection scheduled with a different adjuster.

entries.denialText: [Danny Ticktin (Roof-ER) 2024-11-19] I write to you today as our mutual client's information as well as the Partial Repair approval for 5 shingles on each slope from Nationwide we had on file came across my desk. So we scheduled our Field Technician to perform a repair attempt to remove and replace one individual shingle in order to demonstrate the irreparability of this old and brittle roof. As can be clearly seen in the video and pictures, the homeowner's roof sustained additional damage during the attempted repairs. Thus confirming a failed brittle test for the shingles, indicating irreparability as a major concern for the roof as a whole. With this fact established, your estimate as it currently stands would not actually fix the damages, but would in fact further compromise the integrity of the roof as a whole. Therefore I have gone ahead and written up an estimate that accurately reflects the full scope of work necessary to make our mutual client whole again. These photos clearly demonstrate the brittleness of this roof, and moving forward with further patch repairs will inevitably do further unavoidable damage to surrounding shingles. Please review these findings and revise your estimate/scope of work accordingly, so we can begin to move forward with the necessary full roof replacement. [Ryan Lacy (Nationwide Claims Associate) 2024-11-21] Following up with you with feedback on the video and photos you had submitted on Cheng / Vienna, VA. Nationwide is of the opinion that the video and photos do not effectively prove that this roof is in an unrepairable condition, as there did not appear to be much - if any - damage present following the repair attempt. We will stand on the attached current estimate at this time. Manming and Maria: I do need to issue the initial payment on this claim today per Nationwide's claim handling guidelines. I will be sending you a $3,470.61 check now. Total: $5,091.25, Deductible: $1,000.00, Nationwide Owes: $4,091.25, Phase 1: $3,470.61, Phase 2: $620.64. [Reese Samala (Roof-ER Director) 2024-11-21] Hello Ryan, Could you clarify how the photos and videos did not prove that the roof is not repairable? In the video and photos taken by our specialist, I can clearly see the typical indicators of a roof that are not repairable. If there is any additional information that I could provide, please let me know. [Ryan Lacy (Nationwide Claims Associate) 2024-11-22] Good Morning Reese, Thank you for reaching out on Cheng / Vienna, VA. We're not seeing the clear creasing and/or granule loss to the surrounding shingles in the video, in particular, that would allow us to determine that the roof is not in a repairable condition. The shingles appeared to be pliable enough to us to proceed with the repair per shingle on this file.
entries.counterText: Could you clarify how the photos and videos did not prove that the roof is not repairable? In the video and photos taken by our specialist, I can clearly see the typical indicators of a roof that are not repairable.

entries.source: Gmail: Liberty Mutual Claim 059304720-01 — Coverage Explanation & Settlement
entries.denialText: [Brett Beatty (Liberty Mutual / Property Field Claim Resolution Specialist III, FL Adjuster License G043685) 2025-05-20] This email contains very important information about your claim. Please read and review the attached documents and all information below in its entirety. Attached is a copy of 'Coverage Payment Letter' and 'Approved Estimate'. The 'Coverage Payment Letter' will explain what is covered for this claim and/or explain what is not covered under the terms of the policy. In addition, it will explain the breakdown of the payment. The 'Approved Estimate' will break down in detail what is covered and how much it will cost. The pricing we use in our estimate adheres to industry market standards for most licensed contractors to complete the job. But if your chosen contractor's estimate is more they can submit for a supplement request for anything essential that was not included in our 'Approved Estimate', by going to https://property-supplements.libertymutual.com/. Please do not submit any estimates to us from contractors that you do not choose to complete the repairs. Once completed, our Property Resolution Team will review the request and try to reach a reasonable agreement. The assigned Resolution Specialist will work directly with your contractor and may require additional documentation to support their request. 'Recoverable Depreciation' refers to the difference between the Replacement Cost Value and the Actual cash value of the covered loss. We will release up to this amount only when proof of repairs or replacement has been received. [Jermaine Perkins (Roof-ER Operations) 2025-05-20] Tagged you both in File #10283 - Jeffrey Combs. Cody missed the meeting with the HO and Adjuster from yesterday. HO already received an email from INS Co. I asked the HO to send the information he rec'd from his INS Co. HO does not understand/am able to read all of the information received, but he sees that there was a full approval. Please contact HO with review, confirmation, and next steps.

[Shamanda Houston (USAA Claims Service) 2024-10-23] Hello, This correspondence is regarding the following USAA claim: USAA policyholder: Andre Jones, Claim number: 015826705-800. I have attached the estimate per your request. You may reply to this message. If you need to provide documentation, you can attach documents to your email. We can't guarantee the security of any medical, financial or other personally identifiable information sent by email.
